Heiss_Region_Definition: Global cerebral cortical FDG rCMRGlc average reported by Heiss; exact cortical template components are not enumerated in the paper.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human A1C; Human A13; Human A14; Human A19; Human A23; Human A24; Human A25; Human A29-A30; Human A32; Human A35-A36; Human A35r; Human A38; Human A40; Human A43; Human A44-A45; Human A46; Human A5-A7; Human FI; Human Idg; Human Ig; Human ITG; Human LEC; Human M1C; Human MEC; Human MTG; Human Pir; Human Pro; Human S1C; Human STG; Human TF; Human TH-TL; Human V1C; Human V2; Human AON
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: A1C = Primary auditory cortex (Transverse temporal gyrus; cerebral cortex); A13 = Brodmann area 13 (Posterior intermediate orbital gyrus; caudal orbital frontal cortex); A14 = Brodmann area 14 (Gyrus rectus; medial orbitofrontal cortex); A19 = Brodmann area 19 (Superior occipital gyrus; areas 19 and MT / visual association cortex); A23 = Brodmann area 23 (Caudal cingulate gyrus / posterior cingulate cortex); A24 = Brodmann area 24 (Rostral cingulate gyrus; ventral medial frontal cortex / anterior cingulate region); A25 = Brodmann area 25 (Subcallosal gyrus; subgenual/subcallosal medial frontal cortex); A29-A30 = Brodmann areas 29 and 30 (Retrosplenial cingulate cortex / retrosplenial region); A32 = Brodmann area 32 (Rostral gyrus; dorsal medial frontal cortex / dorsal anterior cingulate-medial prefrontal region); A35-A36 = Brodmann areas 35 and 36 (Perirhinal gyrus / perirhinal cortex); A35r = Rostral subdivision of Brodmann area 35 (Perirhinal cortex); A38 = Brodmann area 38 (Temporal pole / temporopolar cortex); A40 = Brodmann area 40 (Supramarginal gyrus; inferior parietal lobule); A43 = Brodmann area 43 (Parietal operculum / gustatory cortex); A44-A45 = Brodmann areas 44 and 45 (Inferior frontal gyrus; ventrolateral prefrontal cortex / Broca-region cortex); A46 = Brodmann area 46 (Middle frontal gyrus / dorsolateral prefrontal cortex); A5-A7 = Brodmann areas 5 and 7 (Superior parietal lobule; posterosuperior/dorsal parietal cortex); FI = Frontal agranular insular cortex (Frontal insula / agranular insular cortex); Idg = Dysgranular insular cortex (Long insular gyri; HCA ontology label shown as agranular insular cortex); Ig = Granular insular cortex (Short insular gyri); ITG = Inferior temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); LEC = Lateral entorhinal cortex (Anterior parahippocampal gyrus); M1C = Primary motor cortex (Precentral gyrus); MEC = Medial entorhinal cortex (Anterior parahippocampal gyrus, posterior part); MTG = Middle temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); Pir = Piriform cortex (Paleocortex); Pro = Area prostriata (Rostral cuneus / cuneus cortex, near the parieto-occipital boundary); S1C = Primary somatosensory cortex (Postcentral gyrus); STG = Superior temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); TF = Temporal area TF (Temporal part of occipitotemporal/fusiform gyrus); TH-TL = Parahippocampal areas TH and TL (Posterior parahippocampal gyrus); V1C = Primary visual cortex (Lingual gyrus / calcarine region); V2 = Secondary / extrastriate visual cortex (Caudal cuneus; peristriate cortex); AON = Anterior olfactory nucleus (Paleocortex / olfactory cortex)
Notes: Heiss reports a global cerebral cortex average, but the paper does not enumerate which cortical template parcels contribute. It explicitly reports hippocampus and insular lobe separately from lobar cortex. Treat this as a broad cortex-level comparison only; paleocortex/olfactory and entorhinal/parahippocampal Siletti regions may not be homologous to the Heiss cortical template without checking the cited cortical-template source.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Lobar cortical VOI corresponding to frontal cortex.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human A14; Human A24; Human A25; Human A32; Human A44-A45; Human A46; Human M1C
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: A14 = Brodmann area 14 (Gyrus rectus; medial orbitofrontal cortex); A24 = Brodmann area 24 (Rostral cingulate gyrus; ventral medial frontal cortex / anterior cingulate region); A25 = Brodmann area 25 (Subcallosal gyrus; subgenual/subcallosal medial frontal cortex); A32 = Brodmann area 32 (Rostral gyrus; dorsal medial frontal cortex / dorsal anterior cingulate-medial prefrontal region); A44-A45 = Brodmann areas 44 and 45 (Inferior frontal gyrus; ventrolateral prefrontal cortex / Broca-region cortex); A46 = Brodmann area 46 (Middle frontal gyrus / dorsolateral prefrontal cortex); M1C = Primary motor cortex (Precentral gyrus)
Notes: Heiss separates the insular lobe from frontal/temporal/parietal lobar rows, so do not include FI/Idg/Ig here. A24/A25/A32 remain unresolved because Heiss does not give the cortical-template boundary rules; these medial wall/cingulate regions may be treated as frontal or cingulate/limbic depending on the template.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Lobar cortical VOI corresponding to parietal cortex.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human A40; Human A43; Human A5-A7
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: A40 = Brodmann area 40 (Supramarginal gyrus; inferior parietal lobule); A43 = Brodmann area 43 (Parietal operculum / gustatory cortex); A5-A7 = Brodmann areas 5 and 7 (Superior parietal lobule; posterosuperior/dorsal parietal cortex)
Notes: Broad lobar match. A43/gustatory-opercular cortex remains a boundary ambiguity because Heiss does not specify whether it is parietal, opercular, or insular in the cortical template. Heiss does separately report insular lobe, so insular parcels should not be included here.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Lobar cortical VOI corresponding to temporal cortex; Heiss reports hippocampus and insula separately.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human A1C; Human A38; Human ITG; Human MTG; Human STG; Human TF
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: A1C = Primary auditory cortex (Transverse temporal gyrus; cerebral cortex); A38 = Brodmann area 38 (Temporal pole / temporopolar cortex); ITG = Inferior temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); MTG = Middle temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); STG = Superior temporal gyrus (Temporal neocortex); TF = Temporal area TF (Temporal part of occipitotemporal/fusiform gyrus)
Notes: Resolved in part by Heiss: Temporal lobe, Insular lobe, and Hippocampus are distinct rows. Use temporal neocortical/gyral Siletti regions only. Do not include hippocampus or insula. Do not include LEC, MEC, A35-A36, A35r, or TH-TL unless you explicitly decide that the Heiss temporal cortical template includes parahippocampal/entorhinal cortex; Heiss itself does not resolve that boundary.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Lobar cortical VOI corresponding to occipital cortex.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human A19; Human Pro; Human V1C; Human V2
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: A19 = Brodmann area 19 (Superior occipital gyrus; areas 19 and MT / visual association cortex); Pro = Area prostriata (Rostral cuneus / cuneus cortex, near the parieto-occipital boundary); V1C = Primary visual cortex (Lingual gyrus / calcarine region); V2 = Secondary / extrastriate visual cortex (Caudal cuneus; peristriate cortex)
Notes: Clean lobar match. Pro/area prostriata lies near the cuneus/parieto-occipital boundary, but Heiss gives no finer cortical-template rule.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Cortical VOI corresponding to insular cortex, reported separately from lobar frontal/temporal/parietal cortex.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human FI; Human Idg; Human Ig
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: FI = Frontal agranular insular cortex (Frontal insula / agranular insular cortex); Idg = Dysgranular insular cortex (Long insular gyri; HCA ontology label shown as agranular insular cortex); Ig = Granular insular cortex (Short insular gyri)
Notes: Heiss treats insular lobe as its own row, separate from frontal/temporal/parietal cortex. Siletti splits insula into FI, Idg, and Ig; use these as a direct many-to-one insular match and do not double-count them in lobar cortical rows.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Allocortical hippocampal VOI reported separately from temporal neocortex.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human CA1R-CA2R; Human CA1R-CA2R-CA3R; Human CA3R; Human DGR-CA4Rpy; Human Sub; Human CA1U; Human CA1-3; Human CA1U-CA2U-CA3U; Human CA2U-CA3U; Human DGU-CA4Upy; Human CA1C-CA3C; Human CA4C-DGC; Human Sub (tail)
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: CA1R-CA2R = Rostral CA1-CA2 hippocampal fields (Body of hippocampus; rostral CA1-2); CA1R-CA2R-CA3R = Rostral CA1-CA3 hippocampal fields (Body of hippocampus; rostral CA1-3); CA3R = Rostral CA3 hippocampal field (Body of hippocampus; rostral CA3); DGR-CA4Rpy = Rostral dentate gyrus / CA4 pyramidal subfield (Body of hippocampus; rostral DG-CA4); Sub = Subicular cortex / subicular complex (Head/tail of hippocampus); CA1U = Uncal CA1 hippocampal field (Head of hippocampus; uncal CA1); CA1-3 = CA1-CA3 hippocampal fields (Caudal hippocampus / hippocampal tail in HCA label); CA1U-CA2U-CA3U = Uncal CA1-CA3 hippocampal fields (Head of hippocampus; uncal CA1-3); CA2U-CA3U = Uncal CA2-CA3 hippocampal fields (Head of hippocampus; uncal CA2-3); DGU-CA4Upy = Uncal dentate gyrus / CA4 pyramidal subfield (Head of hippocampus; uncal DG-CA4); CA1C-CA3C = Caudal CA1-CA3 hippocampal fields (Tail of hippocampus; caudal hippocampus); CA4C-DGC = Caudal CA4 plus dentate gyrus complex (Tail of hippocampus; caudal hippocampus); Sub (tail) = Subicular cortex, hippocampal tail (Tail of hippocampus / caudal subicular complex)
Notes: Heiss separates Hippocampus from Temporal lobe and discusses it as allocortex rather than neocortex. Match to Siletti hippocampal subfields only. Entorhinal/parahippocampal regions such as LEC, MEC, A35-A36, A35r, and TH-TL are not explicitly resolved by Heiss and should not be silently folded into the Heiss hippocampus row.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Section heading in Heiss Table 1, not a measured aggregate region.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: Heiss row is a section heading, not a measured region.
Notes: Do not use in regression. In Heiss Table 1, Forebrain nuclei is a section heading, not a measured aggregate. Use the individually reported rows below it, e.g. caudatum, putamen, nucleus accumbens, pallidum, thalamic/geniculated/subthalamic nuclei, basal forebrain, and amygdala.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Caudate nucleus; Heiss does not subdivide head/body/tail.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: CaB = Body of caudate nucleus (Basal nuclei)
Notes: Heiss reports caudatum without head/body/tail subdivision. Siletti CaB appears to represent caudate body only, so this is not a complete whole-caudate match unless the missing caudate subdivisions are absent from the Siletti comparison or are handled elsewhere.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Putamen.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: Pu = Putamen (Basal nuclei)
Notes: Direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Nucleus accumbens / ventral striatal region.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: NAC = Nucleus accumbens (Basal nuclei / ventral striatum)
Notes: Direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Globus pallidus; Heiss reports whole pallidum rather than internal/external segments.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human GPe; Human GPi; Human Gpe
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: GPe = External segment of globus pallidus (Basal nuclei); GPi = Internal segment of globus pallidus (Basal nuclei); Gpe = External segment of globus pallidus (Basal nuclei; case-variant of GPe in the spreadsheet)
Notes: Heiss reports whole pallidum; Siletti splits into GPe/GPi (and a duplicate 'Gpe' row). Sum or average Siletti subdivisions to compare.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Medial thalamic nucleus/nuclei; Heiss does not enumerate finer thalamic subnuclei.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: MD = Mediodorsal nucleus of thalamus (Medial nuclear complex of thalamus); MD-Re = Mediodorsal plus reuniens nuclei of thalamus (Medial nuclear complex; mediodorsal nucleus plus reuniens/medioventral nucleus)
Notes: Likely maps to mediodorsal/medial thalamic Siletti labels MD and MD-Re, but Heiss reports 'nucleus medial thalami' without enumerating thalamic subnuclei. Confirm against the stereotactic atlas conventions if this row is high leverage.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Lateral geniculate nucleus/body of thalamus.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: LG = Lateral geniculate nucleus/body (Posterior thalamus; visual relay nucleus)
Notes: LGN - direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Medial geniculate nucleus/body of thalamus.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: MG = Medial geniculate nucleus/body (Posterior thalamus; auditory relay nucleus)
Notes: MGN - direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Subthalamic nucleus.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: STH = Subthalamic nucleus (Subthalamic/diencephalic region; listed under thalamus in HCA hierarchy)
Notes: Direct STH match, but note the nomenclature mismatch: Siletti's coarse group places STH under Thalamus, whereas Heiss groups subthalamic nucleus with diencephalic structures rather than thalamus proper. Keep STH as its own subthalamic/diencephalic row, not as part of Heiss medial thalamus.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Basal forebrain VOI; Heiss links this to the nucleus basalis of Meynert but does not enumerate exact VOI composition.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: SI = Substantia innominata and nearby nuclei (Basal forebrain); SEP = Septal nuclei (Basal forebrain; septal nuclear complex)
Notes: Mapping SI plus SEP to Heiss basal forebrain is defensible but not fully specified by Heiss. Heiss links basal forebrain to nucleus basalis of Meynert and cites Mesulam et al. for basal forebrain cholinergic components including septal area, diagonal band, nucleus basalis/substantia innominata, and hypothalamus. Heiss does not enumerate the exact VOI composition.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Amygdaloid body / amygdala.
Siletti_RegionsThatMap: Human BL; Human BM; Human CEN; Human CMN; Human CoA; Human La
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: BL = Basolateral amygdaloid nuclear complex (Amygdaloid complex; basolateral nuclear group / basal nucleus); BM = Basomedial / accessory basal amygdaloid nucleus (Amygdaloid complex; basolateral nuclear group); CEN = Central amygdaloid nucleus (Amygdaloid complex; central nuclear group); CMN = Corticomedial amygdaloid nuclear complex (Amygdaloid complex; corticomedial nuclear group); CoA = Anterior cortical amygdaloid nucleus (Corticomedial nuclear group of amygdala); La = Lateral amygdaloid nucleus (Amygdaloid complex; basolateral nuclear group)
Notes: Heiss whole amygdala; Siletti splits into 6 subnuclei. Sum or weight by subnuclear volume.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Section heading in Heiss Table 1, not a measured aggregate region.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: Heiss row is a section heading, not a measured region.
Notes: Do not use in regression. In Heiss Table 1, Brain stem nuclei is a section heading, not a measured aggregate. Use only the individually measured rows: colliculus inferior, colliculus superior, substantia nigra, and nucleus ruber.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Inferior colliculus of the midbrain tectum.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: IC = Inferior colliculus (Midbrain tectum; auditory midbrain)
Notes: Direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Superior colliculus of the midbrain tectum.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: SC = Superior colliculus (Midbrain tectum; visuomotor midbrain)
Notes: Direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Substantia nigra of the midbrain.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: SN = Substantia nigra (Midbrain); SN-RN = Substantia nigra, red nucleus, and nearby nuclei (Combined midbrain dissection region)
Notes: Direct SN component match, but Siletti also has a combined SN-RN row. Decompose or exclude the combined SN-RN row to avoid double-counting with nucleus ruber.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Red nucleus of the midbrain.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: RN = Red nucleus (Midbrain); SN-RN = Substantia nigra, red nucleus, and nearby nuclei (Combined midbrain dissection region)
Notes: Direct RN component match, but Siletti also has a combined SN-RN row. Decompose or exclude the combined SN-RN row to avoid double-counting with substantia nigra.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Section heading in Heiss Table 1, not a whole-cerebellum measured aggregate row.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: Heiss row is a section heading, not a measured region.
Notes: Do not treat as whole cerebellum. In Heiss Table 1, Cerebellum is a section heading. The reported cerebellar rows are cerebellar cortex, vermis, and nucleus dentatus cerebelli. The abstract's cerebellum value of 29.8 matches the cerebellar cortex value, not a whole-cerebellum aggregate.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Cerebellar cortical VOI.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: CBL = Lateral cerebellar hemisphere / cerebrocerebellum (Cerebellar cortex); CBV = Cerebellar vermis (Cerebellar cortex, medial vermal compartment)
Notes: Heiss reports cerebellar cortex as a measured row. Match to cerebellar cortical Siletti labels CBL and CBV, excluding cerebellar deep nuclei.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Cerebellar vermis.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: CBV = Cerebellar vermis (Cerebellar cortex, medial vermal compartment)
Notes: Direct 1-to-1 match.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Dentate nucleus of the cerebellum.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: CbDN = Cerebellar deep nuclei (Cerebellar nuclear complex)
Notes: Heiss reports dentate nucleus specifically. Siletti CbDN is broader if it aggregates all cerebellar deep nuclei, so this is a dentate-specific subset of the Siletti deep-nuclear category unless Siletti CbDN can be decomposed.

Heiss_Region_Definition: White-matter heading / white-matter category in Heiss Table 1.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: no corresponding Siletti region in this mapping.
Notes: Heiss reports white-matter PET values, but the Siletti region set used here has no corresponding white-matter parcel. Drop from Heiss-Siletti regression.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Centrum semiovale white matter.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: no corresponding Siletti region in this mapping.
Notes: White matter only. No corresponding Siletti parcel in this mapping; drop from Heiss-Siletti regression.

Heiss_Region_Definition: Internal capsule white matter.
Siletti_RegionsExpanded: No Siletti acronym: no corresponding Siletti region in this mapping.
Notes: White matter only. No corresponding Siletti parcel in this mapping; drop from Heiss-Siletti regression.
